label: Placenta
quick_take: Placental / gestational trophoblastic tumours are rare in the West (ASR typically < 0.1) but CI5 shows a surprisingly strong Asian / Indigenous / Sub-Saharan signal that echoes older clinical literature on molar-pregnancy incidence. Almost all countries show decline; the outliers are geography *and* ethnic sub-populations.

findings.title: USA New Mexico American-Indian women, vol III: ASR 8.71, 35× the world median
findings.text: This is by far the top spatial outlier for the site — a historic finding and one that has not been revisited much in recent decades. Worth re-checking with subsequent SEER Native American subgroups.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — USA New Mexico American-Indian women, vol III: ASR 8.71, 35× the world median

findings.title: Vietnam Hanoi women, vol VII (1.85) and vol VIII (2.22): 20× median
findings.text: The Southeast-Asian gestational-trophoblastic-disease belt is visible in CI5 with strong numbers.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Vietnam Hanoi women, vol VII (1.85) and vol VIII (2.22): 20× median

findings.title: Argentina Chaco (1.61), India Poona (2.68), Nigeria Ibadan (3.08)
findings.text: all consistent with the historical geography of molar pregnancy.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Argentina Chaco (1.61), India Poona (2.68), Nigeria Ibadan (3.08)

findings.title: Early-age peak at age band 4–5 (15–24 yr)
findings.text: in virtually every large registry — exactly where gestational trophoblastic disease is biologically expected.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Early-age peak at age band 4–5 (15–24 yr)

findings.title: No signals in men, which is as it should be
findings.text: **No signals in men, which is as it should be.**
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — No signals in men, which is as it should be

findings.text: Very small case counts for almost every registry-volume cell; the magnitude of "top outlier" entries here is sensitive to one or two cases.
Gestational trophoblastic tumours have coding variability across ICD-8/9/10 — some may hide under C79 (metastatic) historically.
Most of the late-volume zero rates reflect genuine rarity plus small registries; do not over-interpret "collapse".
The Vietnam Hanoi + Argentina Chaco + Indian-subcontinent cluster maps the historical geography of hydatidiform mole well — a coherent descriptive piece could revive attention to the topic.

The US Native-American 1970s signal (New Mexico vol III) is interesting because of its extreme magnitude; a quick cross-check with the contemporary Native-American-specific SEER subset would reveal whether it persists.
